title: Personal Injury & Civil Expert Witness | Psychology Direct
description: Psychological and psychiatric injury assessments for personal injury and civil claims. PTSD, trauma, chronic pain reports.

blocks.headline: Get Court-Ready Expert Psychological Evidence Fast
blocks.subtitle: Personal injury and civil claims need expert psychological or psychiatric assessments to quantify psychological harm and support damages. We match the right expert, handle all coordination, and deliver court-ready reports on Legal Aid rates.
blocks.secondaryCtaText: What's Your Case Worth? Use Fee Calculator

blocks.heading: Trauma Assessments That Strengthen Your Case

children.text: Our HCPC-registered psychologists assess the psychological impact of accidents, negligence, and workplace incidents. All are enhanced DBS-checked and experienced in trauma assessment.
Psychology assessments for PI and civil cases include:
Clinical negligence and malpractice impact assessments
Our GMC-registered psychiatrists assess mental health diagnoses, treatment needs, and medical causation. They provide psychiatric opinions that strengthen damages awards and court evidence.
Psychiatry assessments for PI and civil cases include:
Causation opinions (linking injury to mental health)
Chronic pain syndrome and psychiatric comorbidity

Complex cases often benefit from both a psychologist and psychiatrist. A psychologist quantifies psychological impact and functional loss. A psychiatrist provides medical diagnosis and treatment opinions.
Together, they give courts the full picture needed for fair damages assessment. Not sure which you need? Use our tool: Psychologist or Psychiatrist? → /tools/psychologist-or-psychiatrist/

steps.title: Provide Your Case Details
steps.description: Call us or complete our brief form. Tell us about the incident, symptoms, and your deadline. Your Client Manager handles the rest.

steps.title: Receive Matched Experts
steps.description: Within 24 hours, you'll get 2–3 CVs from trauma-experienced experts. All offer Legal Aid rates.

steps.title: Instruct & We Manage Everything
steps.description: Once you select an expert, we coordinate all appointments, liaise with medical professionals, and manage the process.

steps.title: Court-Ready Report
steps.description: Your report is fully quality-checked and proofread. We ensure all legal questions are answered and findings are clear. Standard turnaround is 4–6 weeks; fast-track available.

blocks.heading: Personal Injury & Civil Cases We've Supported

caseStudies.title: RTA PTSD Assessment with Fast Turnaround
caseStudies.summary: A claimant suffering severe PTSD from a road accident needed urgent psychological assessment for damages. We matched a trauma psychologist and delivered the report within 4 weeks.

caseStudies.title: Medical Negligence Dual Assessment
caseStudies.summary: A clinical negligence claim required both psychological and psychiatric assessment of harm from malpractice. We coordinated both experts and delivered comprehensive reports on time.

caseStudies.title: Workplace Bullying & Mental Health
caseStudies.summary: A workplace bullying claim required objective assessment of psychological injury and link to the hostile environment. We provided an occupational psychologist's detailed report supporting damages.

faqs.question: How does a psychological injury assessment support a claim?
faqs.answer: A psychological expert assesses mental health symptoms, diagnoses conditions like PTSD or depression, and evaluates functional impact. They quantify psychological harm and opine on treatment needs, which directly informs damages.

faqs.question: Do I need a psychologist or a psychiatrist?
faqs.answer: A psychologist usually assesses psychological harm and functional impact. A psychiatrist helps if the claimant has medical comorbidities or if medical causation is disputed. Many cases benefit from both.

faqs.question: How long after an incident should an assessment be done?
faqs.answer: Ideally, wait until acute distress has settled (typically 2–6 months post-incident). Early assessments may overestimate severity; delayed assessments may miss acute effects. Your expert will advise on the right timing for your case.

faqs.question: Can assessments be done remotely?
faqs.answer: Yes. Many claimants prefer remote sessions for convenience and comfort. Our psychologists are experienced in remote trauma assessment. Some claimants benefit from in-person assessment instead. The expert will advise on the best approach.

faqs.question: What if the claimant is still receiving treatment?
faqs.answer: Ongoing treatment is common and doesn't prevent assessment. The expert reviews treatment records and assesses current mental state. This actually strengthens evidence of ongoing psychological harm.

faqs.question: How much does a PI psychological assessment cost?
faqs.answer: Costs depend on case complexity, assessment duration, and report scope. Use our fee calculator for an instant estimate, or call us. All quotes include assessment, report writing, and quality assurance. For Legal Aid cases, we work at LAA-set rates.

faqs.question: Will the expert report address damages?
faqs.answer: Yes. Expert reports address diagnosis, severity, functional impact, prognosis, and treatment needs. These directly inform damages. The court decides quantum, but our experts provide the evidence the court needs to assess fairly.

faqs.question: Can I get a report within weeks, not months?
faqs.answer: Standard turnaround is 4–6 weeks. We offer fast-track services for urgent cases. Contact us to discuss your timeline. We often deliver faster when needed.

blocks.heading: Ready to Strengthen Your Case with Expert Evidence?
blocks.bodyText: We'll match you with a trauma expert, handle all coordination, and deliver a court-ready report on your timeline.
